channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rare cyclone weakens to a tropical low weather system as it approaches the Australian east coast
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — A tropical cyclone weakened to a tropical low weather system on Saturday as it approached Brisbane, Australia’s third-most populous city, bringing flooding rain that was expected to lash the coastal region for days.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 22 dead amid gang clashes in Ecuador's largest city
item.description: Gang infighting has left nearly two dozen people dead in Guayaquil, Ecuador's largest city. 14 people were arrested in the aftermath of the Thursday skirmish.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clashes in Syria between government forces and Assad loyalists kill more than 200
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Fighters siding with Syria's new government stormed several villages near the country's coast, killing dozens of men in response to recent attacks on government security forces by loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad, a war monitor said.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 90,000 Palestinians attend the first Friday prayers of Ramadan in Jerusalem
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — In the first Friday prayers of the Muslim holy month of Ramadan, about 90,000 Palestinians prayed at the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound in Jerusalem’s Old City under tight security by Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: IMAD ISSEID and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cyclone Alfred weakens as it closes in on Australia's east coast
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred was downgraded to a tropical low weather system Saturday as it closed in on Brisbane, Australia. Over 19,000 people have been evacuated, and one remains missing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In 2015, Obama committed the US to achieving UN global goals by 2030. Trump just rejected the goals
item.description: In 2015, then-President Barack Obama committed the United States to achieving newly adopted U.N. global goals by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Court orders South Korean President Yoon freed from jail for martial law trial
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol was ordered to be released from jail by a South Korean court after the legal period for his formal arrest expired before his indictment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine supporters focus on hope and resilience as US relations sour and war carries on
item.description: Religious leaders, scholars and diplomats have gathered at the University of Notre Dame in Indiana for a conference on Ukraine studies with a theme of hope.
item.dc:creator: Peter Smith, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization after giving world a sign of his frailty
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis hit the three-week mark Friday in his hospitalization for double pneumonia, in stable condition, receiving therapy but not necessarily improving, after giving the world a tangible indication of just how frail and sick he is.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and MARIA CHENG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump weighs new sanctions on Russia, days after pausing military aid and intel sharing with Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is “strongly considering” levying new sanctions and tariffs on Russia for its war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump seeking new nuclear deal with Iran with letter to supreme leader
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he has written a letter to Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, calling on the country to negotiate a new nuclear deal with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Takeaways from the EU's landmark security summit after Trump said Europe must fend for itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders are trumpeting their endorsement of a plan to free up hundreds of billions of euros to inject into their defense budgets after the Trump administration warned that the continent must look after its own security, including Ukraine, in future.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: The UN is reporting that four boats with immigrants from East Africa capsized off of Yemen leaving 186 missing. Two crewmembers were rescued.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian foreign minister: Trump tariffs 'pretext' to annexation
item.description: Canadian Foreign Minister Mélanie Joly on Thursday claimed that President Trump's tariffs against Canada — the U.S.'s largest trading partner — are just a "pretext" for the administration's wish to eventually annex the nation.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 100 people died of cholera in less than a month in Sudan's White Nile State
item.description: Nearly 100 people have died of cholera in two weeks since the waterborne disease outbreak began in Sudan’s White Nile State.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis still receiving oxygen therapy, will not read Sunday prayers: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still receiving oxygen therapy and will not be able to read Sunday prayers, according to the Vatican. The pontiff is now entering his fourth week in Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polish leader Tusk plans large-scale military training for all adult males to boost reserves
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk says his government is working on a plan to prepare large-scale military training for every adult male in response to the changing security situation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Gera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy confirms Ukraine will attend US peace talks in Saudi Arabia, 1 week after Oval Office clash
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has confirmed that his team will participate in peace talks with their US counterparts in Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police in Bangladesh use batons and tear gas to disperse rally by banned Islamist group
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Police used batons and stun grenades on Friday to disperse thousands of members of the banned Hizbut Tahrir group as they marched near Bangladesh’s main Baitul Mokarram Mosque in the country’s capital.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Greenland, Panama fiercely reject Trump's ambitions in address to Congress
item.description: Greenland and Panama made clear to President Donald Trump that his ambitions in their countries are unwelcome after he against addressed them in a speech to Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese hackers, officials charged in cyberespionage campaign
item.description: The hacking by workers of I-Soon was done in some cases at the direction of China's Ministry of Public Security.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canada may halt electricity exports to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Premier Doug Ford has threatened to cut off electricity exports to the US if President Trump's tariffs are enacted.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Arab countries are working on a postwar plan for Gaza
item.description: Arab foreign ministers met in Cairo on Monday for talks focusing on Egypt's plan to rebuild the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, hoping to counter President Donald Trump’s proposal to expel Palestinians from the coastal enclave and take it over.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US aircraft carrier arrives in South Korea after North Korea test-fired missiles
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A U.S. aircraft carrier arrived in South Korea on Sunday in a show of force, days after North Korea test-launched cruise missiles to demonstrate its counterattack capabilities.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation
